stat_of_week: 40 - Sugar Land Skeeters scored 40 runs this week, the most explosive offensive output of the young season, powering an 8–1 demolition of Hiroshima Radiation
thriller_note: BaseballPro77 escaped with a one-category win despite a bloated 5.28 ERA and just 2 home runs, while Benchwarmers outperformed them in several offensive categories but couldn't overcome the pitching deficit — a loss that keeps Benchwarmers searching for consistency heading into a date with Riverside Roughnecks.
key_storyline: Wisconsin Rapids Reapers delivered the most dominant performance of Week 2, sweeping the Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs 9–0 behind a suffocating 2.48 ERA, 0.84 WHIP, and a league-leading 75 strikeouts — the latter tying the season record set by the Abbotsford-Colby Twins in Week 2. Meanwhile, the Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs posted zero stolen bases, only 3 home runs, and a .270 OBP, making it one of the most lopsided offensive efforts of the season. The Wonderdogs must regroup fast, as their multi-week struggles suggest deeper roster problems that a flurry of waiver moves this week may or may not solve.
lucky_reason: Benchwarmers posted the second-lowest strikeout total among losers and ran a 4.89 ERA, yet still nearly stole the matchup, with a luck delta of +0.58 — the highest in the league — indicating their near-win far exceeded what their underlying stats deserved.
unlucky_reason: General Hospital posted a sparkling 2.10 ERA — the lowest ERA by a winner this season — along with a .363 OBP and strong run production, yet their expected win share of 0.92 far outpaced their actual 0.40, meaning their dominant stats were wasted against a brutal schedule alignment.
records_broken: The season record for most strikeouts by a team in a week was tied: Wisconsin Rapids Reapers posted 75 strikeouts, matching the previous record set by the Abbotsford-Colby Twins (77, Week 2) — wait, Reapers' 75 does not surpass 77, so no new strikeout record. No season records were broken this week.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dominant 8–1 win, league-best 40 runs, and consistent top-tier category production across two weeks | up
2. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Historic 9–0 sweep with elite pitching metrics; 75 Ks and 0.84 WHIP are borderline untouchable | up
3. Riverside Roughnecks | Explosive 38-run week and 7–3 win signal a team hitting its stride after a slow Week 1 | up
4. North Correa | Oneil Cruz and Ohtani carried a 7–3 win; pitching depth remains a slight concern but offense is elite | same
5. General Hospital | Posted the best ERA of any winner this season (2.10) and wins despite tough luck; real contender | up
6. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Yordan Alvarez's .600 OBP and team-best 80 IP anchored a 6–4 win; pitching staff is a weapon | same
7. BaseballPro77 | Scraped out a 5–4 win with ugly pitching; two straight wins but underlying stats are volatile | down
8. Hiroshima Radiation | Carlos Correa and Schwarber showed up but the offense collapsed late; 8–1 blowout loss hurts | down
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison was brilliant but the team still fell 7–3; aggressive waiver activity shows urgency | same
10. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge and James Wood couldn't overcome poor pitching in a 6–4 loss; floor feels limited | down
11. Buttville Skidmarks | Bryan Reynolds had a nice week but a 7–3 beatdown exposes real pitching and depth concerns | same
12. Benchwarmers | Corbin Carroll is producing but the pitching staff is a liability; barely lost what they should have won easily | same
13. Canberra Wombats | Altuve (.577 OBP) and Henderson were stars but 59.1 IP and weak saves cost them the match | same
14. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A 9–0 shutout loss with zero stolen bases and a .270 OBP; desperate waiver moves needed immediately | down
